name: Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park Loop
name_original: Priego de Córdoba, Carcabuey – ruta circular
description: This difficult 31.8-mile mountain biking route in the Sierras Subbéticas offers challenging terrain and panoramic views near Priego de Córdo
region_intro: Mountain biking around Priego De Córdoba offers diverse terrain within the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park, characterized by dramatic limestone mountains, deep ravines, and extensive olive groves. The region is known for its natural springs, earning it the moniker "City of Water," and features a network of trails suitable for various skill levels. The Vía Verde de la Subbética, a converted railway line, provides a long, accessible path through the scenic landscape. Best mountain bike trails around Priego De Córdoba The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park Loop, a difficult 31.8 miles (51.2 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 12 minutes to complete, traversing the heart of the natural park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is View of Pico Bermejo – Puerto del Cerezo loop from Priego de Córdoba, a difficult 28.4 miles (45.7 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs and panoramic views of the surrounding mountains and valleys. Local mountain bikers also love the Fuenfría Spring – Fuente del Rey loop from Priego de Córdoba, a 35.3 miles (56.8 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including natural springs, often completed in about 5 hours 11 minutes. Mountain biking around Priego De Córdoba is defined by limestone mountains, extensive olive groves, and the Vía Verde de la Subbética. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Priego De Córdoba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Priego De Córdoba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Priego de Córdoba?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails around Priego de Córdoba, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Priego de Córdoba?
A: The trails around Priego de Córdoba cater to various abilities. You'll find 2 easy routes, 31 moderate routes, and 18 difficult routes, ensuring options for beginners to experienced riders.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, there are easy mountain bike trails available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6879216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Uclés loop from Priego de Córdoba&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a moderate route that offers a good balance for those looking for a less strenuous ride while still enjoying the scenery.

Q: Can I find challenging mountain bike routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the region feature substantial climbs. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17230535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Cerezo – View of Pico Bermejo loop from Las Lagunillas&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 49.1 km route with over 1500 meters of elevation gain, offering a demanding experience and panoramic views.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that feature natural springs or water features?
A: Yes, Priego de Córdoba is known as the 'City of Water' due to its numerous natural springs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31465518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuenfría Spring – Fuente del Rey loop from Priego de Córdoba&lt;/a&gt; is a 56.8 km difficult trail that specifically leads through varied landscapes including these natural springs, such as the famous Fuente del Rey.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Priego de Córdoba?
A: You can expect diverse terrain, from the dramatic limestone mountains and deep ravines within the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park to extensive olive groves. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986646361/sierras-subbeticas-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 51.2 km trail, traverses the heart of this varied landscape.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes in Priego de Córdoba are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17230353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pico Bermejo – Puerto del Cerezo loop from Priego de Córdoba&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 45.7 km difficult circular path offering challenging climbs and panoramic views.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often pass through the stunning Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park. You can encounter impressive geological formations, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1045186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Limestone formations and caves in the Bailón Canyon&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also home to several waterfalls, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3153745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorreras de la Fuenseca&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5082947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Chorreras Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or villages accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the picturesque village of Zuheros, with its impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361618" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zuheros Castle&lt;/a&gt; perched on a rock, is located within the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park and serves as an excellent starting point for outdoor activities. The Vía Verde de la Subbética, a converted railway track, also connects various municipalities and showcases the region's diverse scenery and historical viaducts.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Priego de Córdoba?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging climbs, and the stunning natural beauty of the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park and its numerous natural springs.

Q: Is it possible to access mountain bike trails using public transport?
A: While specific public transport links directly to trailheads can vary, the Vía Verde de la Subbética, a 52-kilometer path converted from an old railway track, is generally accessible and connects various municipalities, making it a good option for reaching parts of the trail network without a car.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for mountain biking in the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for mountain biking on established trails within the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park. However, if you plan to ascend Pico de la Tiñosa, the highest point in the province, a permit is required for hiking, so it's advisable to check local regulations if your route takes you into sensitive areas.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Priego de Córdoba, Carcabuey – ruta circular?
faqs.answer: This challenging mountain bike route is approximately 51.2 kilometers long with over 1,080 meters of elevation gain. For experienced riders, it typically takes around 4 to 5 hours to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route traverses the characteristic karst landscape of the Sierras Subbéticas, featuring limestone mountains and deep gorges. You can expect a varied mix of surfaces, including asphalted farm roads, forest paths, gravel roads, and technical rocky trails. Be prepared for significant climbs, some with gradients exceeding 17%, and challenging descents.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Priego de Córdoba, Carcabuey – ruta circular?
faqs.answer: While there isn't one single designated trailhead parking mentioned, you can typically find parking in or around the towns of Priego de Córdoba or Carcabuey, which serve as excellent starting points for this circular route. Look for public parking areas within these towns.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best seasons for mountain biking this route are generally spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these times, the weather is milder and more pleasant for riding. Summers can be very hot in Andalusia, and winters might bring colder temperatures and occasional rain.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to ride in the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and ride this route within the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park. However, always respect local regulations and private property signs.

faqs.question: What notable sights or landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers several points of interest. You'll pass by the historic Caliphal Bridge of Carcabuey, enjoy views from the Adarve of Priego de Córdoba, and see the impressive Fuente del Rey. The Ermita del Calvario is another religious building you might encounter.

faqs.question: Is the Priego de Córdoba, Carcabuey – ruta circular suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, this route is explicitly classified as a difficult mountain bike ride. It requires very good physical fitness and prior mountain biking experience due to its length, significant elevation gain, and technical sections. It is not recommended for beginners or families with young children.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially overlaps with several other paths. You'll find sections that are part of the Andalucía - North Route (Provincia de Córdoba), Carcabuey - Los Villares, and Camino del Hoyo, among others.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter in the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park is rich in biodiversity. Keep an eye out for various birds of prey, including majestic golden eagles and griffon vultures soaring overhead. The area also boasts diverse flora, with wild herbs like thyme and rosemary filling the air.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Priego de Córdoba, Carcabuey – ruta circular?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact route are not detailed, generally, dogs are permitted in the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park. However, it is always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety on challenging terrain and near roads.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this challenging mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should pack essential mountain biking gear, including a well-maintained bike, helmet, gloves, and appropriate cycling attire. Carry plenty of water (at least 2-3 liters), energy snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), a first-aid kit, and navigation tools (like a GPS device or the komoot app). Sunscreen and sunglasses are also highly recommended.
